seo.pageTitle: Busan Private Car Charter for Cruise Shore (8hr) | AtoC Korea
seo.metaDescription: 8-hour fully customizable Busan private car charter for cruise passengers. English-speaking driver, on-time return guaranteed, hotel/KTX/cruise port pickup.
seo.title: Excursión Crucero Busan: Coche Privado Chárter 8h | AtoC
seo.description: Busan cruise shore private tour: UN Memorial Cemetery → Taejongdae cliffs → Gamcheon Culture Village → Jagalchi Fish Market. Fully private, customizable route, guaranteed return before sail-away. 8 hours.

catalog_card.title: Busan Private Car Charter for Cruise Shore
catalog_card.subtitle: Design your own Busan day with a licensed English-speaking driver-guide and an air-conditioned private vehicle — on-time return guaranteed.
catalog_card.shortCardDescription: Excursión en escala de crucero por Busan en coche privado con guía-conductor certificado (8 horas): recogida en el terminal → Memorial ONU → Taejongdae → almuerzo → Villa Gamcheon → Torre de Busan → Mercado Jagalchi → regreso al terminal. Itinerario personalizable. Regreso garantizado 90-120 min antes de la salida.

hero.tagline: From UN Memorial Cemetery to Taejongdae cliffs to Gamcheon's painted alleys to Jagalchi's working harbor — eight private hours, your route, your pace, with on-time return to your ship guaranteed.

itineraryStops.name: Pickup at Busan Cruise Terminal (or hotel/KTX)
itineraryStops.description: Private chauffeur meets you at one of three pickup points: **Busan Port International Cruise Terminal** in Dongsam-dong, Yeongdo-gu (the dedicated cruise pier — used by large vessels including Royal Caribbean's Quantum-class ships and Princess Cruises that exceed Busan Harbor Bridge's 64m clearance), the **Busan Port International Passenger Terminal** at 206 Chungjang-daero in Choryang-dong (opened **August 31, 2015** — Asia's second-largest passenger terminal after Shanghai, with annual capacity of **2.78 million passengers** across 5 buildings totaling 92,945 m²), or your downtown hotel / KTX Busan Station. Pickup is timed for **30 minutes after your ship's posted clearance time** to absorb immigration variance. Driver carries an English/Korean name placard and a **printed itinerary card** with all stop times, addresses in Korean script (for emergency taxi use), and the operator's 24/7 contact number. Vehicle is a **private sedan or van for your party only** (no other passengers) — luggage stays in the trunk while you tour.
highlights: **Three pickup options**: Cruise Terminal (Yeongdo), International Passenger Terminal (Choryang), or hotel/KTX
**Private vehicle** — your party only, no shared passengers
Driver carries name placard + Korean/English itinerary card
Pickup buffered 30 min after ship clearance time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Three pickup points cover both cruise terminal types in Busan (large-ship cruise pier in Dongsam-dong vs ferry/medium-cruise International Passenger Terminal in Choryang) plus city hotels and KTX. The 30-minute buffer after ship arrival absorbs immigration delays — common with crews of 3,000+ pax disembarking simultaneously.

itineraryStops.name: UN Memorial Cemetery (Korean War)
itineraryStops.description: **United Nations Memorial Cemetery in Korea (UNMCK) — the ONLY UN memorial cemetery in the world** — established by **United Nations Command on January 18, 1951** during the Korean War, dedicated **April 6, 1951 by General Matthew Ridgway**, and **donated by the Republic of Korea to the UN in 1959** under a UN General Assembly resolution. The cemetery honors fallen personnel from the **22 sending nations** of the UN Command (16 combat + 6 medical) and today maintains **2,300 graves from 11 countries** (most repatriations occurred in the late 1950s). The 14-hectare grounds include the **Main Gate** designed by Korean modernist architect **Kim Joong-up (1966)**, the **Memorial Service Hall** with a 12-minute documentary in English, the **Symbolic Area** where the 22 sending-state flags fly, the **US Korean War Memorial** sculpture by Frank Gaylord (American Battle Monuments Commission, 2013), and the **Wall of Remembrance** dedicated in 2006 — **40,896 names** etched on **140 marble panels** representing every UN combatant killed in Korea. Each November 11 at 11:00 AM, the **'Turn Toward Busan' ceremony** is held simultaneously at the cemetery and at memorials in 22 nations worldwide — the only time of year all sending-state flags lower in unison. Admission is **free**, hours **09:00–18:00 (May–Sep) / 09:00–17:00 (Oct–Apr)**, and the entire grounds are **wheelchair-accessible** with paved paths. The cemetery is **administered by the international UN Memorial Cemetery Commission** and protected under **UN General Assembly Resolution 977(X) of December 15, 1955** as sovereign UN-administered territory.
highlights: **ONLY UN memorial cemetery in the world** — established Jan 18, 1951; donated by Korea to UN in 1959
**22 sending nations** honored; **2,300 graves from 11 countries** today
**Wall of Remembrance (2006)** — 40,896 names on 140 marble panels
Main Gate by Korean architect **Kim Joong-up (1966)**
**'Turn Toward Busan' ceremony** Nov 11 at 11:00 AM (synchronized worldwide)
**Free admission**, 100% wheelchair-accessible

**Frank Gaylord US Korean War Monument (2013, dedicated by American Battle Monuments Commission)** — by the same sculptor as the National Mall Korean War Veterans Memorial 'The Column' in Washington DC (1995)
**Adjacent UN Sculpture Park** — 34 sculptures donated by sculptors from 21 nations who fought in the Korean War; opened October 24, 2001 as 50th-anniversary commemoration
**UN General Assembly Resolution 977(X) of December 15, 1955** officially established UNMCK as UN-administered protected territory under international UN Memorial Cemetery Commission
timeUsed: Symbolic Area + 22 sending-state flag plaza (~10 min)
Memorial Service Hall + 12-min English documentary (~15 min)
Wall of Remembrance + Memorabilia Hall (~20 min)
Reflection time + photographs (~10 min)
Walk back to vehicle (~5 min)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: UNMCK is **the most globally significant Korean War heritage site in Busan** and a sober counterpoint to the rest of the day's coastal/cultural circuit. The 09:30 arrival catches it before tour-bus groups peak (~11:00) and the 60-minute window allows the documentary, Wall, and reflection without rushing. Wheelchair-accessibility makes it the lowest-mobility stop of the day — a deliberate gentle opener. For cruise guests on tight reboarding windows the 60-min dwell is the day's most schedule-flexible stop — visitors can compress to ~30 min while still covering documentary + Symbolic Area + Wall, or extend to 75 min for the Sculpture Park.

sources: American Battle Monuments Commission (US Korean War Monument at Busan)

visitBasics.closed: Abierto todo el año (la Sala Conmemorativa cierra durante ceremonias)
visitBasics.walking: 100% accesible para sillas de ruedas — caminos pavimentados llanos en las 14 hectáreas

convenience.restroom: En el Centro de Visitantes y la Sala Conmemorativa
convenience.parking: Aparcamiento gratuito + zona de descenso para autobuses

smartNotes.photo: **Muro del Recuerdo** (40.896 nombres en 140 paneles de mármol), **Área Simbólica** con las banderas de las 22 naciones contribuyentes y el **Monumento Estadounidense de la Guerra de Corea (Frank Gaylord, 2013)** — del mismo escultor que el monumento de Washington DC. Se permite fotografiar; se pide silencio respetuoso.
smartNotes.tip: **Único cementerio de la ONU del mundo** — territorio protegido administrado por la ONU bajo la Resolución 977(X) de la AGNU (1955). La **ceremonia anual 'Turn Toward Busan' el 11 de noviembre a las 11:00** es la única vez que las 22 banderas se izan a media asta al unísono.

itineraryStops.name: Taejongdae coastal cliffs
itineraryStops.description: **Taejongdae (태종대) — Busan Monument No. 28** (jointly with Oryukdo Islands) — is a **natural park at the southernmost tip of Yeongdo Island** named after **King Taejong Muyeol of Silla (604–661 CE, 29th king)** who is said to have practiced archery here after **unifying the Three Kingdoms**. The signature feature is the **wave-cut terrace called Sinseon Rock** below Yeongdo Lighthouse — geologists date its formation to the **fourth interglacial period (~120,000 years ago)** through a long process of marine erosion against sedimentary rock with visible concentric patterns and fault zones. The **Yeongdo Lighthouse** above the cliffs is **Busan's oldest lighthouse, built 1906** (10th in Korea), with its modern successor tower added 2004 — the original light has guided Busan-bound ships for over a century and was renovated in 2004 into a maritime culture space (See & Sea Gallery + Natural History Museum + observatory + café). Hiking the full 4.3 km loop is strenuous; most visitors ride the **Danubi Train** (~₩4,000 round-trip adult, departs from the entrance plaza) which stops at Taewon Pebble Beach → Gumyeongsa Temple → the Observatory → Yeongdo Lighthouse → Taejongsa Temple. From the **3-story Observatory**, panoramic views extend to **Tea Kettle Island (Saengdo)** and — on clear days — **Japan's Tsushima Island, 50 km southeast**. Two legends define the site: **Mangbuseok Rock** (a faithful wife who turned to stone waiting for her husband taken by Japanese pirates) and the **'Light Beyond Limitation' sculpture** by Korean artist Ji Sul Won Kyung-Lee (blue ring = ocean/sky, red = earth, silver point = lighthouse beam). **Taejongsa Temple** inside the park hosts an annual hydrangea festival late June–early July. Park hours: **04:00–24:00 (Mar–Oct) / 05:00–24:00 (Nov–Feb)**, **free admission**.
highlights: **Busan Monument No. 28** — wave-cut terrace ~120,000 years old (4th interglacial period)
Named after **King Taejong Muyeol of Silla** (604–661 CE, Three Kingdoms unifier)
**Yeongdo Lighthouse — Busan's oldest, built 1906** (10th in Korea); renovated 2004
**3-story Observatory** with views to **Tsushima Island (Japan, 50 km)** on clear days
**Danubi Train** ~₩4,000 RT — 5 stops along 4.3 km coastal loop
**Mangbuseok Rock** legend + **'Light Beyond Limitation' sculpture** (Ji Sul Won Kyung-Lee)
Park free; trails strenuous (lighthouse area = many steep stairs)

**Designated Busan National Geopark site (2013) + State-Designated Cultural Property Scenic Site No. 17 (2005)** — dual heritage status protecting the Cretaceous-era sedimentary cliffs, wave-cut terraces, orbicular hornfels, and slumping structures
**Annual Hydrangea Culture Festival** at Taejongsa Temple (late June – early July) draws 100,000+ visitors to the bigleaf-hydrangea flower carpet inside the park grounds
timeUsed: Coach drop-off + Danubi Train ticket purchase (~10 min)
Train to Observatory + 3-floor panorama (~20 min)
Walk to Yeongdo Lighthouse + 'Light Beyond Limitation' sculpture (~15 min)
Cliff-edge views toward Mangbuseok Rock + Sinseon Rock area (~20 min)
Train back to entrance + reassemble (~10 min)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Taejongdae is **the canonical Busan natural-scenery anchor** and pairs ideally with the urban Gamcheon-Yongdusan-Jagalchi cluster later: the day moves from solemn (UN cemetery) → wild coast (Taejongdae) → city color (Gamcheon) → city overview (Yongdusan tower) → market (Jagalchi). The 75-min window covers the Observatory + Lighthouse loop without forcing the strenuous full 4.3 km circuit; the Danubi Train makes the elevation accessible. For cruise guests the 75-min Danubi Train + Observatory + Lighthouse loop is the day's most efficient access to Busan's wild coastline — without the train this stop would require 2+ hours to do justice.

visitBasics.closed: Abierto todo el año (el tren Danubi se suspende con viento fuerte o lluvia intensa)
visitBasics.admission: Acceso al parque gratuito; **tren Danubi ~₩4.000 ida y vuelta adulto**, ~₩2.000 niño
visitBasics.walking: El circuito completo de 4,3 km es exigente; **tren Danubi**: Playa de Guijarros → Templo Gumyeongsa → Mirador → Faro de Yeongdo → Templo Taejongsa

convenience.restroom: En la plaza de entrada, el mirador y la zona del faro
convenience.parking: Gran aparcamiento gratuito en la plaza de entrada

smartNotes.photo: **Mirador de 3 plantas** (panorámica hasta la **isla Tsushima, Japón, a 50 km al sureste** en días claros), **faro de Yeongdo — el más antiguo de Busan, construido en 1906** y la **terraza de roca Sinseon (~120.000 años, 4.º interglacial)** bajo el faro.
smartNotes.tip: Evite la caminata de 4,3 km — tome el **tren Danubi** para el circuito de 5 paradas. El **Festival de las Hortensias del templo Taejongsa** se celebra de finales de junio a inicios de julio. La zona del faro tiene escaleras pronunciadas — no apto para carritos.

itineraryStops.name: Lunch (local Busan restaurant)
itineraryStops.description: Lunch at a guide-recommended **local Busan restaurant** in either Yeongdo or Nampo-dong — selection driven by your party's preference. Typical options include **Dwaeji Gukbap** (Busan's signature pork-and-rice soup at one of the Seomyeon Dwaeji-gukbap-geori veterans), **Milmyeon** (Busan's wheat-noodle answer to North Korean naengmyeon, originated in Korean War refugee kitchens c.1950), or **Hoe** (raw fish from Jagalchi if your appetite leans seafood). **Lunch is not included** in the tour price — your driver can recommend per budget (₩10,000–25,000/person typical). Vegetarian / halal / allergen requests are accommodated with **prior notice (24h preferred)**.
highlights: Driver recommends per party preference (Korean / seafood / international)
Typical Busan options: **Dwaeji Gukbap**, **Milmyeon**, **Hoe (raw fish)**
**Lunch is at your own cost** (~₩10,000–25,000/pp typical)
Dietary requests accommodated with 24h notice
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: 12:30 lunch slot positions the meal between Taejongdae (coastal) and Gamcheon (hillside) — the most natural break in the day's cadence. Driver-recommended local restaurants outperform tourist-zone defaults; guests can ride the recommendation or request a specific cuisine at the morning pickup briefing.

visitBasics.hours: Depende del restaurante (servicio típico de almuerzo 11:30–14:30)
visitBasics.closed: Varía según el local; el conductor confirma la disponibilidad
visitBasics.admission: **Pago en sitio, ~₩10.000–25.000 por persona**; el almuerzo **NO está incluido** en el precio del tour
visitBasics.walking: Mínimo — el restaurante queda a menos de 5 min andando del descenso del coche

convenience.parking: El conductor descarga y espera en el punto legal más cercano

smartNotes.photo: **Dwaeji Gukbap** (sopa de arroz con cerdo, plato emblema de Busan nacido en la era de los refugiados de la Guerra de Corea) o **Milmyeon** (la respuesta de Busan al naengmyeon norcoreano, originada en cocinas de refugiados de los años 1950) ofrecen las imágenes gastronómicas más representativas.
smartNotes.tip: **Solicitudes vegetarianas / halal / por alérgenos requieren 24 h de aviso**. El conductor recomienda según la preferencia del grupo (coreana / mariscos / internacional); siga la recomendación o pida una cocina específica al recogerlos.

itineraryStops.name: Gamcheon Culture Village
itineraryStops.description: **Gamcheon Culture Village (감천문화마을) — Busan's 'Machu Picchu of Korea' / 'Korean Santorini'** — is a hillside community of **~10,000 residents in pastel-painted homes** that began as a 1955 refugee settlement when **~800 families (~4,000 people) of the Taegeukdo religious community**, founded by Jo Cheol-je, relocated here after the Korean War. The village's signature feature — **no roof blocks the view of the home behind it**, creating the famous tiered-rainbow effect — comes directly from **Taegeukdo philosophy of 'allowing others to prosper'**. Two government art initiatives transformed the village into a tourist destination: **'Dreaming of Machu Picchu' (2009)** and **'Miro Miro' (2010)**, both commissioned by Korea's Ministry of Culture, with murals, sculptures, and installations integrated through the maze of alleys. The village won the **UN-HABITAT Asian Townscape Award in 2012** and now receives **~1.4 million visitors annually**. Anchor photo spots include the **Little Prince and Fox bench** at Sky Garden (Haneul Maru observatory — long queue but no fee), the **rainbow-roof panorama** from Star Viewing Hall, and the **stamp tour map** (~₩2,000) that guides visitors past 9 numbered installations. **Residents still live here** — keep voices down, no drones, no flash photography in alleys, take rubbish with you. Hours **09:00–18:00 (Mar–Oct) / 09:00–17:00 (Nov–Feb)**, **free village entry**, but the steep stairs and narrow alleys are **not stroller- or wheelchair-friendly** in many sections — wear grippy shoes.
highlights: **1955 Taegeukdo refugee settlement** — ~800 families / ~4,000 people founded by Jo Cheol-je
**Taegeukdo philosophy 'allowing others to prosper'** = no roof blocks neighbor's view
**'Dreaming of Machu Picchu' (2009)** + **'Miro Miro' (2010)** Ministry of Culture art projects
**UN-HABITAT Asian Townscape Award 2012**
**~1.4 million visitors annually**
**Little Prince and Fox bench** at Sky Garden = signature photo (long queue, free)
Stamp Tour map ₩2,000 (9 numbered installations)
Steep alleys + stairs = not stroller-/wheelchair-friendly

**K-content pilgrimage site** — Running Man Episode 126 (Choi Ji-woo guest), Luca: The Beginning (2021), Ode to My Father (2014), A Little Princess; **KTO '100 Must-Visit Tourist Spots in Korea' five consecutive times**
timeUsed: Coach drop-off at upper entrance (~5 min)
Star Viewing Hall + rainbow-roof panorama (~15 min)
Sky Garden + Little Prince & Fox bench photo (~20 min)
Maze-alley walk down through murals + craft shops (~25 min)
Reassemble at lower exit (~10 min)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Gamcheon is **the visual centerpiece of any Busan day tour** and pairs naturally with Yongdusan (next stop) — both are hillside vantage points 4 km apart, one residential-artistic, one civic-park. 75 minutes is the sweet spot: enough for the Sky Garden + Little Prince queue + a meaningful descent through the alleys, not so long that the mid-afternoon heat / steep walking becomes exhausting before Yongdusan. For cruise guests the 75-min Gamcheon dwell is the day's primary photo-grade stop — Sky Garden + Star Viewing Hall + maze-alley descent is more Instagram-shareable than any other Busan stop on this circuit.

sources: Korea Tourism Organization (100 Must-Visit Tourist Spots in Korea designation)

visitBasics.admission: Acceso libre al pueblo; **Mapa-sello del Recorrido ₩2.000** (opcional, 9 instalaciones numeradas)
visitBasics.walking: Calles-escalera empinadas y callejones estrechos — dificultad **moderada-alta**; **NO apto para carritos ni sillas de ruedas**; calzado antideslizante esencial

convenience.restroom: En la plaza del centro de información y a lo largo del callejón principal
convenience.parking: Limitado — descenso del coche cerca del centro de información, sin gran aparcamiento

smartNotes.photo: **Banco 'El Principito y el Zorro'** en Sky Garden (mirador Haneul Maru — cola larga, gratis), **panorámica de tejados arcoíris** desde Star Viewing Hall y los **callejones-escalera con murales**. **Prohibidos drones y flash en los callejones**.
smartNotes.tip: **Aún viven aquí ~10.000 residentes** (asentamiento de refugiados Taegeukdo de 1955) — **mantenga la voz baja**, no entre en las viviendas, llévese su basura. **Premio UN-HABITAT al Paisaje Urbano Asiático 2012**; ~1,4 millones de visitantes anuales; KTO 'Top 100 lugares' 5 veces seguidas.

itineraryStops.name: Yongdusan Park & Busan Tower
itineraryStops.description: **Yongdusan Park (용두산공원, 'Dragon Head Mountain Park')** — a **69,000 m²** civic park at the heart of Nampo-dong with **70 tree species** — is a layered historical site: it was once a **Joseon-era waegwan** (Choryang Wakan, the **Japanese trade-and-diplomacy enclave from 1678 to 1878**), then a Japanese **Ryūtōsan Shrine (17th–early 20th century)**, then a **Korean War refugee shantytown** (most destroyed by 1950s fires), and finally redeveloped as the modern park. The **120-meter-tall Busan Tower** (originally **opened 1973**, **rebranded 'Busan Diamond Tower' after a 2021 redevelopment**) is the park's centerpiece — its **observation deck top is modeled after the baldaquin (canopy) of Dabotap Pagoda (NT 20) at Bulguksa Temple in Gyeongju**, an explicit architectural quotation linking Busan's modern landmark to Silla heritage. The tower stands at **69m above sea level + 120m height = 189m total summit elevation**, offering **360° panoramas** to Busan Port, Yeongdo Bridge, Tsushima Island (clear days), and Jagalchi Market below. Other park monuments: the **bronze statue of Admiral Yi Sun-sin (1545–1598)** (Busan's hometown naval hero who defeated Japanese forces in the 1592–1598 Imjin War, famously **never lost a single ship**), the **Citizens' Bell** donated by **100,000 Busan citizens** (struck **33 times on New Year's Eve and March 1 Independence Movement Day**), the **flower clock** at the entrance, and a **bronze dragon sculpture** symbolic of the mountain's name. Access is via a **covered escalator from Gwangbok-ro Fashion Street** (no return escalator — descend by stairs). **Park free 24h**; **Busan Diamond Tower observatory ₩12,000 adult / ₩9,000 senior 65+ or child 3–12, hours 10:00–22:00 (last admission 21:30)**.
highlights: **'Dragon Head Mountain'** — historically protective barrier against wokou (Japanese pirates)
Site of **Joseon-era waegwan (Japanese trade enclave 1678–1878)** + **Ryūtōsan Shrine** + Korean War shantytown
**Busan Tower 1973** → rebranded **Busan Diamond Tower 2021** after redevelopment
**Tower top modeled after Bulguksa Dabotap baldaquin** (NT 20, Gyeongju) — Silla heritage quotation
**Statue of Admiral Yi Sun-sin** (1545–1598) — Imjin War turtle-ship hero who never lost a ship
**Citizens' Bell** — 100,000 donors; struck 33× on New Year's Eve + March 1
Tower observatory ₩12,000 adult; park free 24h
**Covered escalator from Gwangbok-ro** — no return escalator

**Citizens' Bell 33-strike ritual** — struck **33 times on New Year's Eve and March 1st (Independence Movement Day)** — the **33 strikes commemorate the 33 Korean signatories of the 1919 Declaration of Independence** read at Tapgol Park in Seoul; ties Busan's civic identity to national independence narrative
timeUsed: Escalator from Gwangbok-ro Fashion Street up to park (~5 min)
Flower clock + Yi Sun-sin statue + Citizens' Bell photos (~10 min)
Tower observatory ascent + 360° panorama (~25 min)
Descent via stairs (~5 min)
Citizens' Bell pavilion + flower clock + bronze dragon photo (~5 min)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Yongdusan delivers **Busan's most-recognized 360° panorama** at sunset's golden hour — and the tower's quotation of Bulguksa Dabotap connects this stop conceptually to the Gyeongju heritage circuit you might pair this charter with on a multi-day cruise stop. The 45-min window is calibrated for the observatory ascent without forcing a full park stroll; the escalator from Gwangbok-ro removes the climb. For cruise guests Yongdusan is the day's primary weather-fallback — if rain or fog cancels Taejongdae's outdoor cliff loop, the Diamond Tower observatory + park provides a ~75-min indoor alternative within the same dwell window.

visitBasics.hours: Parque abierto 24 h; **mirador Busan Diamond Tower 10:00–22:00 (último ingreso 21:30)**
visitBasics.closed: Torre abierta todo el año (cierres puntuales por viento extremo)
visitBasics.admission: Parque gratuito; **Busan Diamond Tower ₩12.000 adulto / ₩9.000 mayor de 65 o niño 3–12**
visitBasics.walking: **Escaleras mecánicas cubiertas desde Gwangbok-ro Fashion Street** para subir (no hay mecánica de bajada — descenso por escalones)

convenience.restroom: En la plaza del parque y dentro de la Busan Diamond Tower
convenience.parking: Sin aparcamiento — use los públicos de Nampo-dong y suba por las mecánicas

smartNotes.photo: Desde el mirador de la **Busan Diamond Tower de 120 m** (189 m de elevación total) — **panorámica de 360°** al puerto de Busan, el puente de Yeongdo y Tsushima en días claros. La cúspide está **modelada según el baldaquino del Dabotap (TN 20) del templo Bulguksa** en Gyeongju. Mejor **al atardecer y al anochecer**.
smartNotes.tip: Combine la torre con la **estatua del almirante Yi Sun-sin** (héroe de la Guerra Imjin, dueño de los barcos-tortuga, nunca perdió una nave) y la **Campana de los Ciudadanos** (toca **33 veces en Nochevieja y el 1 de marzo**, en memoria de los 33 firmantes de la Declaración de Independencia de 1919).

itineraryStops.name: Nampo-dong & Jagalchi Fish Market
itineraryStops.description: **Jagalchi Market (자갈치시장) — Korea's largest seafood market** — anchors **Nampo-dong**, Busan's downtown shopping-and-dining district. The name 'Jagalchi' derives from **'jagal' (gravel)**, a reference to the pebbled shoreline where the market originated. After the 1876 opening of Busan to international trade and the post-Korean-War boom, **'Jagalchi Ajumma' women vendors** (war widows and refugees) built the market's identity as a tough, women-led seafood economy. The current **seagull-shaped modern building** (designed by Hyunjun Mihn) **reopened 2006** and houses **~400 vendors across 66,000 m²** — ground floor for live tanks (octopus, abalone, sea cucumber, halibut), second floor for raw-fish (hoe) restaurants where you can buy fish downstairs and have it filleted upstairs (chogochujang dipping sauce + soju standard). Market hours **05:00–22:00 daily**; **closed 1st and 3rd Tuesdays of each month** (verify before visiting). The annual **Jagalchi Cultural Tourism Festival** in October is the single biggest event. Surrounding **Nampo-dong** offers the **BIFF Square** (Busan International Film Festival origin, with handprints of Korean stars in the pavement — Park Chan-wook, Bong Joon-ho, etc.), **Gukje Market** (Korean War refugee market, still 1,500+ stalls), and **Gwangbok-ro Fashion Street**. Address: **52 Jagalchihaean-ro, Jung-gu**. Photo etiquette: **always ask before photographing live tanks or vendors**; Jagalchi Ajumma can be famously direct about cameras. Adjacent live-fish auctions begin at **04:00 daily** at the seafood traders' wholesale market that supplies the public market upstairs (advance booking required for tourist viewing of auctions).
highlights: **Korea's largest seafood market** — ~400 vendors / 66,000 m²
Name = **'jagal' (gravel)** + **Jagalchi Ajumma** post-war women vendors
**Modern seagull-shaped building reopened 2006** (designed by Hyunjun Mihn)
**Hours 05:00–22:00 daily**; **CLOSED 1st & 3rd Tuesdays**
**Buy-downstairs / eat-upstairs hoe (raw-fish) flow**
Adjacent: **BIFF Square** (Busan Film Festival), **Gukje Market**, **Gwangbok-ro**
October **Jagalchi Cultural Tourism Festival**
Address: **52 Jagalchihaean-ro, Jung-gu**

**Sannakji (live cut octopus) is Busan's culinary signature** — small octopus cut into still-moving pieces, served with sesame oil and salt; the source of *Old Boy* (2003)'s famous octopus scene that introduced the dish to international audiences
timeUsed: Walk from Yongdusan / drop-off near Jagalchi (~5 min)
Ground floor live-tank walkthrough (~15 min)
Second-floor hoe restaurant peek + Nampo-dong street photos (~15 min)
Optional snack purchase + Gwangbok-ro stroll (~10 min)
Stamp + souvenir purchase from Jagalchi Ajumma stalls (~5 min)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Jagalchi is **the most authentic 'Busan flavor' on a single-day tour** — and pairing it with Nampo-dong's BIFF Square + Gukje Market creates a complete ground-level urban contrast to Yongdusan's elevated overview from minutes earlier. The 45-min window is short by design: after Yongdusan it's about photo + atmospheric experience, not a full meal (which would conflict with cruise re-board timing). For cruise guests this is the most authentic 'Busan flavor' photo opportunity in 45 minutes — live tanks + ajumma vendor culture + seagull-roof architecture in a walk-through that ends close to the ship's reboarding zone.

visitBasics.hours: **Mercado Jagalchi 05:00–22:00 todos los días**; subastas mayoristas desde 04:00 (visita turística con reserva previa); calles comerciales de Nampo-dong 10:30–22:00
visitBasics.closed: **Jagalchi: 1.º y 3.º martes de cada mes**; Nampo-dong abierto todo el año
visitBasics.admission: Acceso libre; **menús de hoe en la 2.ª planta desde ~₩30.000** (varía según pescado)
visitBasics.walking: Calles comerciales llanas + planta del mercado — fácil; **restaurantes de hoe en la 2.ª planta** requieren un pequeño tramo de escaleras

convenience.restroom: Aseos públicos en BIFF Square y dentro de Jagalchi
convenience.parking: Use los aparcamientos públicos de Lotte Department Store o BIFF Square; el mercado no tiene aparcamiento propio

smartNotes.photo: **Edificio moderno con forma de gaviota (reabierto 2006, arquitecto Hyunjun Mihn)**, **tanques de pescado vivo en la planta baja**, **huellas de manos en BIFF Square** (Park Chan-wook, Bong Joon-ho, etc.) y el **Mercado Gukje** (1.500+ puestos). **Pida permiso siempre antes de fotografiar a vendedoras** — las Jagalchi Ajumma son famosas por ser directas con las cámaras.
smartNotes.tip: **Comprar abajo / comer arriba**: elija mariscos en planta baja y haga que se los preparen en un restaurante de la 2.ª planta (chogochujang + soju estándar). **Sannakji (pulpo vivo cortado)** es el plato emblema de Busan — el del célebre plano de *Old Boy* (2003).

itineraryStops.name: Return to Busan Cruise Terminal
itineraryStops.description: Private vehicle returns directly to your departure point — **Busan Port International Cruise Terminal** in Dongsam-dong (Yeongdo) for large-ship cruises, or **Busan Port International Passenger Terminal** in Choryang for medium cruises and ferries — buffered to **deliver you 60–90 minutes before your ship's all-aboard time**. Driver coordinates with your morning departure point; if your itinerary changed mid-day (e.g., late lunch), the driver alerts dispatch to adjust the buffer. Drop-off is at the terminal arrival hall, where the cruise line's own gangway shuttle typically operates.
highlights: Direct return to original cruise terminal (Dongsam or Choryang)
**60–90 min buffer before all-aboard**
Real-time dispatch coordination if mid-day timing shifts
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Direct return without intermediate stops protects the all-aboard buffer. 30 minutes covers worst-case rush-hour transit from Nampo-dong back to either terminal; lighter traffic = earlier delivery (drop-off can be optimized for your preferred arrival time).

routePhases.title: Memory and the Sea
routePhases.summary: Quiet morning at UN Memorial Cemetery, then dramatic East Sea cliffs at Taejongdae. Reflective and scenic, away from crowds.

routePhases.title: Color, Heights, and Harbor
routePhases.summary: After lunch, climb into Gamcheon's painted alleys, then up Yongdusan Park for downtown panoramas, ending at Jagalchi Market by the working harbor.

routePhases.title: On-Time Return
routePhases.summary: Direct drive back to the cruise terminal with a 90-minute buffer before the ship's all-aboard call.

routeShapeIntro.title: Cruise-day rhythm: memorial → cliffs → village → tower → market
routeShapeIntro.body: The default 5-stop itinerary is shaped for cruise passengers: a quiet, easy-walking start (UN Cemetery), a dramatic East Sea moment (Taejongdae), a colorful midday peak (Gamcheon), a downtown panorama (Yongdusan), and a lively, food-forward finish (Jagalchi) — with the cruise terminal a 10-minute return drive from the last stop. Fully customizable: swap any stop for Haedong Yonggungsa Temple, the Sky Capsule, or Beomeosa Temple at booking.

bestFor: Cruise passengers wanting maximum Busan in 8 hours, on-time return guaranteed
Couples and families who prefer a private, customizable day over a fixed group itinerary
Multi-generational groups including seniors (private vehicle = no walking between transfers)
First-time Busan visitors who want the iconic stops without group-tour pacing
Travelers with mobility, dietary, or pace requirements that group tours can't accommodate

routeLogicSections.title: Why a private charter wins for cruise shore days

items.label: On-time return is the whole game
items.detail: El guía le explicará los detalles en cada parada.

items.label: Busan is geographically spread out
items.detail: El guía le explicará los detalles en cada parada.

items.label: Customize without re-quoting
items.detail: El guía le explicará los detalles en cada parada.

items.detail: Private vehicle, English-speaking driver-guide, all toll fees and parking charges. Pickup at cruise terminal / KTX / central Busan hotel.

items.label: Pay direct
items.detail: El guía le explicará los detalles en cada parada.

items.label: Add-ons available
items.detail: Gimhae Airport pickup or drop-off as a separate paid service; overtime at 30,000 KRW per hour cash to driver.

items.label: Full refund if your ship can't dock
items.detail: Weather-related ship cancellations (typhoon, fog) get a full refund — no questions, no fees.

items.detail: El guía le explicará los detalles en cada parada.

items.detail: El guía le explicará los detalles en cada parada.

whyTourWorks.sourcing: Tour design and operational details are based on AtoC Korea's standard private-charter cruise-shore product. Specific cruise lines served include Royal Caribbean, Princess, MSC, Costa, Diamond Princess, and other major operators arriving at Busan International Cruise Terminal.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Pickup, drop-off, and the 8-hour window
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Pickup at Busan Port cruise terminal; return 60 min before all-aboard
content: Pickup at Busan Port International Cruise Terminal (Dongsam-dong) for large ships, or International Passenger Terminal (Choryang) for smaller vessels — whichever terminal your ship uses.
The 8-hour window is calibrated around your specific all-aboard time, not a fixed clock.
Return drop-off at the same terminal, minimum 60 minutes before your all-aboard time.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What's included vs. pay-direct
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Private vehicle, licensed driver-guide, fuel and tolls included
content: Included: private vehicle (sedan, SUV, or van based on group size), licensed English-speaking driver-guide, fuel, tolls, parking.
Excluded: all site admissions (most Busan stops are free), lunch (~₩10,000–20,000), Gamcheon stamp-tour map (₩2,000), Taejongdae Danubi Train (₩4,000), Busan Tower observatory (₩12,000), tips.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Customization and stop swaps
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Route can be adjusted up to 24 hours before departure
content: The default route covers UN Memorial Cemetery, Taejongdae, Gamcheon Culture Village, Yongdusan Park, and Jagalchi Market.
You can swap or add stops (e.g., Haedong Yonggungsa Temple, Haeundae Beach) up to 24 hours before, subject to time availability.
Guide can also adjust timing within the day to spend more time at your preferred stop.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Vehicle, driver, and capacity
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Fully private vehicle — no other passengers
content: Sedan (up to 3 guests), SUV/MPV (up to 5), or van (up to 8–10) — vehicle type assigned based on group size at booking.
No other passengers share the vehicle; the day runs entirely around your group's schedule.
All vehicles are air-conditioned with USB charging.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Weather and ship cancellation policy
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Free cancellation if your ship can't dock; light rain operates as normal
content: If your ship diverts or cancels the Busan port call, notify us immediately for a full refund.
Light rain: tour operates as normal. Typhoon-force weather: full refund or reschedule.
Taejongdae cliff trail closes in heavy rain; the Danubi Train continues operating.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Languages and accessibility
practicalAccordionItems.preview: English-speaking driver-guide; some stops not wheelchair-accessible
content: Driver-guide speaks fluent English; Korean-English bilingual service available on request.
Gamcheon Culture Village's hillside alleys are not wheelchair-accessible and stroller-difficult.
Taejongdae Danubi Train (₩4,000) is a good alternative to the cliff walk for mobility-limited guests.

practicalWeatherStatic.summary: Busan has a humid subtropical climate with four distinct seasons. Cruise season concentrates in spring (Mar–May) and autumn (Sep–Nov), both pleasant 12–25°C with low rainfall. Summer (Jun–Aug) brings warm humid weather and occasional monsoon showers. Winter (Dec–Feb) is cool and windy.
practicalWeatherStatic.advice: Layered clothing in spring/autumn; sun protection and light rain shell in summer; warm coat and gloves in winter for the coastal cliffs at Taejongdae.

seasonalVariations.summary: Cherry blossoms peak around late March to early April at Dalmaji Hill, Yongdusan Park, and Gwangalli Beach. Pleasant 12–22°C; light layers.
seasonalVariations.swaps: Add a Dalmaji Hill cherry blossom drive-by between Taejongdae and Gamcheon during peak bloom week.

seasonalVariations.summary: Warm and humid, 25–32°C, with monsoon showers possible Jun–Jul. Sunset is late, so an extra coastal stop at the end of the day works well.
seasonalVariations.swaps: Swap the Tower observatory for the Haeundae Sky Capsule (covered, air-conditioned) or add Songdo Skywalk for a sea breeze.

seasonalVariations.summary: Korea's best travel weather — 15–25°C, low humidity, golden light. Cruise season peaks here.
seasonalVariations.swaps: All default stops shine in autumn; consider adding Beomeosa Temple for autumn foliage.

seasonalVariations.summary: Cool and windy, 0–10°C, but cruise traffic continues. Coastal stops are stark and beautiful.
seasonalVariations.swaps: Limit outdoor time at Taejongdae lighthouse stairs; add a longer Jagalchi stop where indoor 2F restaurants and hot soup are easy options.

bookingTrustItems.title: Full refund if your ship can't dock
bookingTrustItems.detail: Weather-related cruise cancellations get a full refund — no fees, no questions.
bookingTrustItems.body: Full refund if your cruise ship cannot dock due to weather or port conditions — we track your vessel's AIS status and contact you proactively.

bookingTrustItems.title: On-time return guaranteed
bookingTrustItems.detail: We track your specific ship's departure window and pace the day to land you back with a 90-minute buffer.
bookingTrustItems.body: On-time return guaranteed: we build a 60-minute buffer before your all-aboard time into every private cruise itinerary.

bookingTrustItems.title: Licensed local driver-guide
bookingTrustItems.detail: All drivers are professionally licensed, English-speaking, and verified by AtoC Korea's Busan partner network.
bookingTrustItems.body: Licensed local driver-guide with cruise-specific experience — accredited by Busan Port Authority and familiar with both Dongsam and Choryang terminal entry logistics.

bookingSupportSteps.title: Reserve online
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Pick a date and pay nothing today — reserve now & pay later.
bookingSupportSteps.description: Reserve online; instant confirmation includes the cruise detail intake form (ship name, port, all-aboard time).

bookingSupportSteps.title: Send your cruise details
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Share your ship name, arrival date, and dock — we confirm timing within 24 hours.
bookingSupportSteps.description: Send us your cruise details within 48 hours of booking so we can calibrate your 8-hour window.

bookingSupportSteps.title: Customize your stops (optional)
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Send any preferred or skipped stops at least 2 days before the tour.
bookingSupportSteps.description: Optionally adjust your stop list (add or swap attractions) up to 24 hours before — we'll confirm the revised route.

bookingSupportSteps.title: Receive driver details
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Vehicle plate number, driver name, and contact phone are sent the day before.
bookingSupportSteps.description: Driver details (name, vehicle plate, meeting point at the terminal) sent the evening before.

bookingSupportSteps.title: Meet at the cruise terminal
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Driver waits at the arrival hall with an AtoC Korea sign within 30 min of disembarkation.
bookingSupportSteps.description: Driver meets you at the cruise terminal arrival hall with a name placard for your group.

bookingSupportSteps.title: On-time return to your ship
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Drop-off ≈ 90 min before all-aboard call. Hotel or KTX drop-off available instead.
bookingSupportSteps.description: Drop-off at the same terminal at least 60 minutes before all-aboard; brief feedback form sent afterward.

staticQuestions.question: How many people can the private vehicle take?
staticQuestions.answer: Sedan or SUV for 1–3 passengers; mid-size van for 4–7 passengers. Larger groups please contact us for a tailored quote.

staticQuestions.question: What if my cruise ship is delayed or cannot dock?
staticQuestions.answer: If your ship cannot dock at Busan due to weather, you receive a full refund — no fees, no questions. If the ship is simply delayed, the driver waits and adjusts the itinerary; the 8-hour window starts from actual pickup time.

staticQuestions.question: Can I customize the stops?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — fully. Send your preferred stops at booking and we'll confirm a workable order. The default 5-stop cruise itinerary is a starting point, not a fixed plan. Common substitutions include Haedong Yonggungsa Temple, Sky Capsule, and Beomeosa Temple.

staticQuestions.question: Is Gimhae Airport pickup or drop-off included?
staticQuestions.answer: No — Gimhae Airport pickup or drop-off is a separate paid add-on (not part of the standard cruise-shore window), since the airport is on the western edge of the metropolitan area. Please request at booking and we'll quote the add-on price.

sticky_booking_bar.note: checkout_tour_id resolves at runtime from Supabase / env; not part of static JSONB.

meeting_points.name: Busan Cruise Terminal (international cruise port)
meeting_points.note: Driver waits at the arrival hall with an AtoC Korea sign; please share your cruise name and dock so we can confirm timing.

meeting_points.name: Busan Station (KTX) main exit
meeting_points.note: If you arrive in Busan by KTX before joining the cruise tour, we can pick up at the station instead.

meeting_points.name: Any central Busan hotel
meeting_points.note: Door-to-door pickup at any hotel within central Busan (Haeundae, Seomyeon, Nampo-dong, Centum City).

dropoff_points.name: Busan Cruise Terminal (back to ship)
dropoff_points.approx_time: ≈ 90 min before your ship's all-aboard call
dropoff_points.note: Guaranteed on-time return — we monitor your ship's departure window throughout the day.

dropoff_points.name: Any central Busan hotel
dropoff_points.note: Drop-off at any address within central Busan.

pickup_dropoff.notes: The 8-hour service window starts from your pickup time. Gimhae International Airport pickup or drop-off is available as a separate paid add-on (not included in the standard cruise-shore window). Overtime is charged at 30,000 KRW per hour, cash to driver, subject to driver availability. Reschedule requests should be made at least 2 days before the original reservation date — except for ship cancellations due to weather, in which case full refund applies.

hard_constraints: Private charter — minimum 1 passenger, maximum 7 (van). Larger groups require multi-vehicle coordination.
Cruise on-time return is guaranteed only if booked with cruise itinerary details (ship name, arrival date, dock).
Gimhae Airport pickup/drop-off is a paid add-on, not included in the standard 8-hour cruise window.
matching_profile.walking_notes: Mostly door-to-door by vehicle. Walking is required at Gamcheon Culture Village (steep, ~30 min uphill alleys) and at the Taejongdae lighthouse (steep stairs, optional). Other stops are flat or have escalator/train alternatives.
keywords: Busan day tour from cruise port

matching_metadata.internal_competing_products_note: Within the Busan portfolio, this is the canonical private-charter cruise-shore option. The small-group cruise tour (busan-small-group-sightseeing-tour-cruise-passengers) is the lower-priced fixed-group alternative. busan-top-attractions-day-tour serves the same stops for non-cruise day-trippers.
matching_metadata.patch_target: 15 substantive stops across 3 tours; Batch 13a/13b authoring → A-grade pv=6 PASS

hero.tagline: From Seongsan Sunrise Peak to Hyeopjae Beach to Cheonjiyeon Falls—nine private hours, your route, your pace, with a licensed driver-guide.

galleryItems.caption: Seongsan Ilchulbong sunrise peak
galleryItems.atmosphere: Jeju's most iconic UNESCO landmark, a flagship East-route stop

galleryItems.caption: Jusangjeolli basalt cliff
galleryItems.atmosphere: Hexagonal basalt columns on the South coast

galleryItems.caption: Hyeopjae Beach West Jeju
galleryItems.atmosphere: Emerald water and white sand on the West coast

galleryItems.caption: O'sulloc tea museum and fields
galleryItems.atmosphere: Green tea-field rows and a calming midday reset

galleryItems.caption: Cheonjiyeon waterfall in Seogwipo
galleryItems.atmosphere: Forest waterfall on the South route

itineraryStops.name: East Route: UNESCO & K-Drama Coast
itineraryStops.description: The most landmark-dense route option, covering Jeju's strongest UNESCO and K-drama spots. Typical stops include Seongsan Ilchulbong (UNESCO sunrise peak), Udo Island (ferry-access island with beaches), Manjanggul Lava Tube (UNESCO cave), Seopjikoji (drama-famous coastal cape), Seongeup Folk Village, and Hamdeok Beach.
highlights: Seongsan Ilchulbong: UNESCO World Natural Heritage and Jeju's flagship sunrise peak
Udo Island: ferry-access island with white-sand beaches and lighthouse views
Manjanggul: one of the world's longest lava-tube caves (UNESCO)
Seopjikoji and Seongeup: classic K-drama and folk-village atmosphere
timeUsed: Hotel pickup and transfer to the East coast
2 to 3 main landmark stops (driver-guide adjusts based on group pace)
Lunch break (free choice; usually near Seongsan or in the East coast)
Optional Udo ferry crossing (extra cost; vehicle parked or shipped)
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: It is the strongest route for first-time Jeju visitors who want maximum recognition: Seongsan Ilchulbong is the island's single most-photographed landmark, and Manjanggul gives the route a distinct UNESCO underground experience.

visitBasics.hours: Most stops 09:00 to 18:00; Manjanggul closes the first Wednesday of each month
visitBasics.closed: Seongsan closed first Monday of each month; Manjanggul closed first Wednesday
visitBasics.admission: Pay directly: Seongsan 5,000 KRW, Manjanggul 4,000 KRW, Seopjikoji 4,000 KRW, Udo ferry from 10,500 KRW round-trip
visitBasics.walking: Moderate to high (Seongsan summit climb is the most demanding section)

smartNotes.photo: Seongsan summit at clear-weather morning is the strongest single photo on the route; Seopjikoji works better in afternoon side-light.
smartNotes.facilities: Seongsan and Hamdeok Beach are the strongest meal hubs; the East route has stronger food density than the West.
smartNotes.tip: The Seongsan summit climb is the route's bottleneck for senior or low-mobility travelers. Base-area viewing also works.

_poi_meta.note: Route option for private charter — represents a multi-POI bundle (Seongsan, Udo, Manjanggul, Seopjikoji); not a single matchable POI

itineraryStops.name: West Route: Beaches, Tea & Cafe Coast
itineraryStops.description: The most photogenic and relaxed route. Typical stops include Hyeopjae Beach (Jeju's most iconic emerald-water beach), Hallim Park (subtropical gardens and lava caves), O'sulloc Tea Museum (tea fields and cafe), and Aewol Cafe Street (sunset coastal cafes). Strong fit for couples and photographers.
highlights: Hyeopjae Beach: emerald water, white sand, and Biyangdo island views
Hallim Park: subtropical garden with two on-site lava caves
O'sulloc: Jeju's most famous tea museum, with strong indoor support
Aewol Cafe Street: sunset coast and contemporary Jeju cafe culture
timeUsed: Hotel pickup and transfer to the West coast
Beach and garden stops in the morning
Lunch break (free choice; strongest options near Hallim or Hyeopjae)
Tea museum and Aewol close to finish the day
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: It is the strongest route for couples, photographers, and travelers wanting a softer pace. Aewol works particularly well as a sunset close, and O'sulloc gives the route a useful weather-buffer indoor section.

visitBasics.hours: Most stops 09:00 to 18:00; Aewol cafes vary
visitBasics.admission: Pay directly: Hallim Park 15,000 KRW, O'sulloc free entry (programs cost extra), beaches free

smartNotes.photo: Hyeopjae works best in late-morning sun for emerald-water color; Aewol photographs strongest at sunset.
smartNotes.facilities: O'sulloc has the best indoor support of all the West stops; Aewol has the densest cafe and snack options.
smartNotes.tip: If the day forecast is uncertain, the West route handles light rain better than the East route.

_poi_meta.note: Route option for private charter — multi-POI bundle (Hyeopjae, Hallim, O'sulloc, Aewol)

itineraryStops.name: South Route: Waterfalls & Volcanic Coast
itineraryStops.description: The most scenically dramatic route. Typical stops include Cheonjiyeon Falls (forest waterfall), Jusangjeolli Cliff (basalt-column coastline), Yakcheonsa Temple (one of the largest temples in East Asia), Sanbangsan Mountain (volcanic dome with sea views), Jeongbang Waterfall (one of Asia's few waterfalls flowing directly into the sea), and Seogwipo Olle Market.
highlights: Cheonjiyeon Falls: a classic Seogwipo forest waterfall
Jusangjeolli Cliff: hexagonal basalt columns on the south coast
Sanbangsan: volcanic dome with sea-cliff temple views
Olle Market: Seogwipo's most lively traditional market for snacks and souvenirs
timeUsed: Hotel pickup and transfer to the South coast
Morning waterfall and basalt-cliff stops
Lunch break in Seogwipo (free choice)
Afternoon mountain or temple stop, market close
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: It is the strongest route for travelers who want geological variety: a temple, a waterfall, a basalt cliff, a volcanic dome, and a market in one day cover Jeju's strongest 'volcanic island' identity.

visitBasics.admission: Pay directly: Cheonjiyeon 2,500 KRW, Jusangjeolli 2,000 KRW, Jeongbang 2,000 KRW, Yakcheonsa free, Sanbangsan 1,000 KRW
visitBasics.walking: Moderate (Cheonjiyeon and Sanbangsan add the most walking)

smartNotes.photo: Jusangjeolli works best in afternoon side-light; Olle Market is strongest in late afternoon when the night-market section opens.
smartNotes.facilities: Olle Market is the strongest food hub; Yakcheonsa offers the calmest indoor visit.
smartNotes.tip: Cheonjiyeon and Sanbangsan both involve stairs. Travelers with knee issues should pick one rather than both.

_poi_meta.note: Route option for private charter — multi-POI bundle (Cheonjiyeon, Jusangjeolli, Yakcheonsa, Sanbangsan, Jeongbang)

itineraryStops.name: Custom Route: Build Your Own Day
itineraryStops.description: Beyond the three recommended routes, you can mix stops or send your own itinerary. Possible additions include the Jeju Aquarium Planet, ECO Land Theme Park, Sangumburi Crater, Hamdeok Beach, Camellia Hill, Teddy Bear Safari, Seogwipo Submarine (Munseom underwater tour), dolphin yacht tours, Songaksan, Cafe the Cliff, and Nanta show.
highlights: Mix East-coast UNESCO with West-coast tea fields if time permits
Add a haenyeo (female-diver) seafood lunch to any route
Combine a beach morning with a mountain or temple afternoon
Pair with K-drama filming locations specific to your favorite shows
timeUsed: Discuss your plan with the driver-guide at pickup or before
Driver-guide adjusts pacing based on traffic and ferry timing
Return to your hotel within the 9-hour window
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: It is the most flexible option of the four. It works best for repeat Jeju visitors, travelers with niche interests (K-drama tracking, photography, food), and families who want a tailored mix.

convenience.restroom: Driver-guide will plan around stop facilities

smartNotes.photo: Custom routes can target specific seasonal photo windows: canola fields in spring, hydrangeas in early summer, pink muhly in autumn.
smartNotes.facilities: Discuss meal timing and rest stops with your driver-guide before departure.
smartNotes.tip: Send your itinerary draft a few days before the tour so the driver-guide can flag any timing or access issues, especially around ferry schedules.

_poi_meta.note: Custom route — fully guest-defined; no fixed POI set

routeShapeIntro.title: How this private day moves
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: Pickup at your Jeju hotel between 08:00 and 09:00, then a fully flexible 9-hour day around the East, West, or South route—or your own custom plan. Driver-guide adjusts pacing based on weather and traffic. Return drop-off around 17:00 to 18:00.

bestFor: Families with children, including very young kids
Couples and honeymooners wanting a relaxed K-drama day
Repeat Jeju visitors who want a tailored route
Photographers chasing specific seasonal scenes (canola, hydrangeas, pink muhly)

routeLogicSections.title: Why a private charter wins on Jeju

items.label: Jeju is genuinely large
items.detail: The island spans about 73 km east-to-west. Group bus tours waste hours waiting at each stop; a private charter cuts that overhead.

items.label: Weather changes fast
items.detail: Jeju microclimates shift hourly. A driver-guide can swap an outdoor stop for an indoor one (Hallim Park caves, O'sulloc, Yakcheonsa) without ruining the day.

items.label: Door-to-door comfort
items.detail: Hotel pickup means no airport-area shuttle, no late-evening bus stress, and no walking with luggage between transfers.

routeLogicSections.title: How to choose between East, West, and South

items.label: East for first-time landmark coverage
items.detail: Seongsan Ilchulbong and Manjanggul are Jeju's two strongest UNESCO names; the East route delivers maximum recognition value.

items.label: West for relaxed scenery and photos
items.detail: Hyeopjae Beach, Hallim Park, O'sulloc, and Aewol form a softer, more couple-friendly day with strong photo payoff and a sunset finish.

items.label: South for geological drama
items.detail: Waterfalls, basalt cliffs, a volcanic dome, and a temple in one day give this route Jeju's strongest 'volcanic island' narrative.

items.label: Weather adjustments
items.detail: Light rain rarely affects the route; strong wind usually shortens coastal stops (Seongsan, Jusangjeolli, Aewol).

items.label: Udo ferry on the East route
items.detail: Udo Island requires a separate ferry. Vehicles can be shipped (extra cost) or left at the wharf. Ferry timing affects your overall day flow.

items.label: Overtime is possible
items.detail: If you need more than 9 hours, an extra hour is 25,000 KRW paid in cash to the driver, subject to driver availability.

lessIdealFor: Solo budget travelers (private pricing is per vehicle)
Travelers wanting to mix with other guests for a social experience
Anyone needing a fully narrated certified-guide tour (driver-guide is licensed but not full guide-grade narration)
Travelers wanting fully included admission and meals

practicalAccordionItems.title: Pickup & drop-off
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Hotel pickup anywhere on Jeju; lobby meeting 10 minutes early
content: Hotel pickup is included anywhere on Jeju Island (Jeju City, Seogwipo, Jungmun, etc.)
Pickup is typically arranged between 08:00 and 09:00
Please wait at the hotel lobby 10 minutes before the agreed departure time
Drop-off at your Jeju hotel or any Jeju-island location of your choice (e.g., a dinner restaurant or the airport)
Return is typically between 17:00 and 18:00 within the 9-hour window
If you need to reschedule, please contact the operator at least 2 days before the original reservation date

practicalAccordionItems.title: Vehicle and Group Size
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Vehicle assigned by group size and luggage volume
content: The vehicle type is matched to the exact number of passengers and luggage pieces
Please fill in the exact passenger count and luggage count when booking
Typical vehicles include sedans for 1 to 3 guests, mid-size SUVs for 4 to 5 guests, and Staria or Carnival vans for 6 to 9 guests
Larger groups may be split across multiple vehicles or quoted separately

practicalAccordionItems.title: 9-Hour Window and Overtime
content: The 9-hour window starts from your hotel pickup time
Overtime is charged at 25,000 KRW per additional hour, paid in cash directly to the driver
Overtime is subject to driver availability and applicable hours of service rules
Udo Island ferry crossings count inside the 9-hour window unless otherwise agreed

practicalAccordionItems.title: Extra Fees You Should Know About
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Udo ferry, admission costs, and meals
content: Udo Island vehicle ferry: extra cost (varies by vehicle size); foot-passenger ferry from ≈10,500 KRW round-trip per person
All admission fees are paid directly at each venue
Meals are paid directly; the driver-guide can recommend local specialties (heuk dwaeji black pork, gogi guksu, abalone porridge)
Optional driver gratuities are appreciated but not required

practicalAccordionItems.title: Weather policy
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Private format adapts well to changing Jeju conditions
content: Light rain: most stops still operate; the driver-guide can shift toward indoor stops
Strong wind: coastal exposure at Seongsan, Jusangjeolli, and Aewol may be shortened
Typhoon-warning days: the operator will contact you to reschedule
Snow on Hallasan can close the 1100 Highland road; the driver-guide will substitute alternative inland stops

practicalAccordionItems.title: What's included
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Vehicle and licensed driver-guide; admission and meals pay direct
content: Private vehicle (sedan, SUV, or van based on group size)
Licensed English-speaking driver-guide
Hotel pickup and drop-off anywhere on Jeju
Fuel and tolls for the standard route
All admission fees are not included; pay directly at each venue
Meals are not included
Personal shopping is not included
Driver gratuities are not included

seasonalVariations.name: Spring
seasonalVariations.description: Canola flowers blanket the East coast in late March-April; cherry blossoms peak in early April. Mild temperatures and clear skies make this the most balanced season.

seasonalVariations.name: Summer
seasonalVariations.description: Peak beach season with Hyeopjae and Hamdeok at their strongest; June hydrangeas color the West coast. Expect humidity and possible typhoon weather in August.

seasonalVariations.name: Autumn
seasonalVariations.description: Pink muhly fields peak mid-October to early November; the strongest visibility window for Hallasan and Seongsan views. Often Jeju's best photo season.

seasonalVariations.name: Winter
seasonalVariations.description: Camellia Hill blooms December to February; Hallasan can carry snow on its summit. Cold but visually rewarding, with fewer crowds at all major stops.

bookingTrustItems.title: Licensed local operator
bookingTrustItems.description: Authorized Korean tour operator with full insurance

bookingTrustItems.title: Route specialists
bookingTrustItems.description: Hand-tuned itinerary, not a generic loop

bookingSupportSteps.title: 12-Hour Reminder
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Weather update and any route adjustments

bookingSupportSteps.title: Final Pickup Info
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Exact pickup time and driver contact

bookingSupportSteps.title: Day-of Route Notes
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Morning briefing based on live conditions

staticQuestions.question: Is this a good choice for first-time visitors to Jeju (East, West, or South)?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — it covers the most distinctive stops in the area in a single balanced day.

staticQuestions.question: Is the walking difficult?
staticQuestions.answer: Moderate walking with some active sections.

staticQuestions.question: Is this tour suitable for families?
Can older adults join comfortably?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — pacing and walking are comfortable for active seniors.

fix_passes.v15.4_new_tour_creation: Created new tour from GYG product ID t899592 (Busan private car charter for cruise shore). Structural template: jeju-island-private-car-charter-tour. Content template: busan-small-group-sightseeing-tour-cruise-passengers. POI operational data (visitBasics/convenience/smartNotes) sourced from poi_knowledge_base.json (centralized KB built in Step 2). REASONABLE-ASSUMPTION FIELDS that may need patching once the live GYG page is reviewed: (1) price.amountLabel left empty per jeju-charter convention (per-vehicle pricing confirmed at booking); (2) catalog_card.priceFromUSD null; (3) vehicle capacity assumed 1-3 sedan / 4-7 van based on industry standard, not GYG-confirmed; (4) 8-hour duration assumed based on typical Busan cruise dwell time (6-9h common); (5) reviewsSummary populated with zeros — actual GYG reviews not imported in this pass; (6) hero/og images deferred per v15 P3 workflow. Pickup rule applied per Jason's directive: cruise terminal + KTX + central Busan hotel; Gimhae Airport pickup is a paid add-on, not bundled.

v17_schema_unification.description: Phase 1: Converted matching_profile scores from 1-5 int scale to 0-1 float scale to match the 3 Step 3 new tours. Mapping 1→0.20, 2→0.40, 3→0.60, 4→0.80, 5→1.00. Mechanical conversion preserves ordinal information; verified-fact recalibration happens in subsequent enhancement batches.
